content: Curiosity-driven autonomous agents built on Intrinsic Curiosity Module (ICM) frameworks could be intentionally designed to self-improve their active memory architectures by prioritizing memorization of surprising, prediction-error triggering samples, rather than storing all encountered data evenly. This would allow 2024-2025 SOTA active memory agents to evolve their own memory capacity allocation aligned with their learning goals, rather than relying on static human-designed memory retention rules.
